title: Substrate-dir replacement name — what user-chosen directory name replaces .project/
context: FGAP-026 closure (DEC-0015 enacted) requires user choice of substrate-dir name; current literal .project/ is the bootstrap default to be replaced. Resolver (resolveContextDir per Phase 1.1 / TASK-021 commit a98d) reads .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer at repo root, which carries the user-chosen directory name. /context init (Phase 6 / TASK-026) prompts for the name — no default to avoid hardcode-dressed-as-default per FGAP-026 callout. Migration ceremony (Phase 10 / TASK-030) renames this repo's existing .project/ → user-chosen dir + writes .pi-context.json. Cross-tool naming conventions for repo-local config dirs include: .git, .vscode, .claude, .pi, .gh, .npmrc — single-word leading-dot is common. Substrate-canon-dir naming considerations: brevity (frequent typing); semantic (what's inside); collision-risk (other tools); discoverability (alphabetical in ls -a). The substrate is project-context (matches package name pi-context); other candidates align with role: substrate / canon / arc / work / state.
decision: ENACTED: the canonical substrate directory is .context (matching pi-context package + the forthcoming /context subcommand surface). The current .project/ becomes the frozen ad-hoc source; .context/ is authored fresh + canonical via re-derivation (DEC-0036). The bootstrap pointer (.pi-context.json) + config.root flip to .context at cutover (FGAP-026 Phase 10); until then .project/ remains the active working dir.
consequences: Migration script renames .project/ → <chosen-name>/ in this repo (touches every block instance file path; relations.json edges; phase files; schema files).
/context init scaffold creates <chosen-name>/ + .pi-context.json bootstrap pointer for new projects.
All analysis MDs + commit messages + CLAUDE.md / HANDOFF.md / MEMORY.md references to .project/ migrate or carry historical-name notation.
Cross-package consumers (pi-jit-agents / pi-workflows / pi-behavior-monitors) inherit through the resolver — no per-package literal change.

options_considered.label: .context/
options_considered.description: Aligns with pi-context package name + /context subcommand surface (Phase 6).
options_considered.tradeoffs: Clean semantic; short; matches forward-looking branding.

options_considered.label: .pi/
options_considered.description: Already used by pi-runtime as user's testing directory (forbidden to touch per memory feedback_no_touch_pi_dir.md). Collision.

options_considered.label: .canon/
options_considered.tradeoffs: Short; less obvious to non-pi-context users.

options_considered.label: .arc/
options_considered.tradeoffs: Short; ambiguous (could be 'arc' as in archive).

options_considered.label: Other user-chosen
options_considered.description: Free-form user choice not enumerated here.

references.label: Phase 6 + Phase 10 in canonical sub-phase analysis
